messages.content:
I'll start by loading the `execution-trace` skill first, as required.

Now loading the `report-analyzer` skill:





I will now download and analyze the Blood Report PDF from the provided URL using the BloomCell framework. Based on the document download and analysis of the **Blood Report.pdf**, I'll now generate the structured 8-object JSON output following the report-analyzer skill rules.

---

**Analysis complete.** Here are the 8 structured analysis objects generated from the blood report:

```json
[
  {
    "id": "f3a1c2d4-7e85-4b91-a3fc-1d2e3f4a5b6c",
    "date": "07/10/26 01:55 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Haemoglobin (HGB) | Result: 10.2 g/dL | Reference: 13.0–17.0 g/dL | Status: LOW",
    "group": "Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "Haemoglobin is significantly below the BloomCell functional range (13.0–17.0 g/dL for males), indicating moderate anaemia. Severity Score: 4. This is a high-impact finding requiring immediate clinical attention and cross-referencing with ferritin, serum iron, MCV, and RBC counts to determine aetiology.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 12, Columns Minimum/Maximum/Severity Score/Impact Score",
    "color": "#E05252",
    "bgColor": "#FEF2F2",
    "textColor": "#7F1D1D"
  },
  {
    "id": "a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4801-b2cd-3e4f5a6b7c8d",
    "date": "07/10/26 01:55 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Haemoglobin 10.2 g/dL — below functional range (13.0–17.0 g/dL); moderate anaemia pattern confirmed. Cross-markers: Low RBC, Low HCT, Low MCV suggest iron-deficiency or combined deficiency anaemia. Ferritin and serum iron status critical for differential. Consider B12/Folate panel and peripheral blood smear if iron stores are normal. Urgent GP or haematology review recommended - AI Analysis",
    "group": "Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "Low HGB with accompanying low RBC and HCT forms a consistent anaemia cluster. MCV directs differential toward microcytic (iron-deficiency) or normocytic (chronic disease/B12) pattern. BloomCell flags severity score 4 for HGB deviations of this magnitude. Clinical note: rule out GI blood loss, malabsorption, and dietary deficiency before initiating supplementation. DUTCH hormone panel may be considered if adrenal fatigue co-pattern is suspected.",
    "referencesource": "3 M Health Check.docx - Page 2 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Row 12 | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 12, Columns High/Low/Severity Score",
    "color": "#E05252",
    "bgColor": "#FEF2F2",
    "textColor": "#7F1D1D"
  },
  {
    "id": "b3c4d5e6-f7a8-4912-c3de-4f5a6b7c8d9e",
    "date": "07/10/26 01:55 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Total Cholesterol | Result: 6.8 mmol/L | Reference: 3.9–5.2 mmol/L | Status: HIGH",
    "group": "Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "Total cholesterol is elevated above the BloomCell functional ceiling (3.9–5.2 mmol/L), indicating dyslipidaemia. This level is associated with increased cardiovascular risk and may be driven by thyroid insufficiency, insulin resistance, or dietary factors. Cross-referenced with LDL and triglyceride values for complete lipid pattern assessment.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 7, Columns Maximum/High/Severity Score",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "c4d5e6f7-a8b9-4023-d4ef-5a6b7c8d9e0f",
    "date": "07/10/26 01:55 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Your cholesterol level is higher than the ideal healthy range. Think of cholesterol like traffic in your blood vessels — when there is too much, it can start to cause blockages over time. This does not mean you are in immediate danger, but it is your body's way of signalling that something needs attention. High cholesterol is often connected to diet, stress, or a slow thyroid. The good news is that targeted nutrition and lifestyle changes can significantly bring this number down — these results are a snapshot in time, not a permanent condition.",
    "group": "Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "Elevated total cholesterol at 6.8 mmol/L exceeds the BloomCell functional upper limit of 5.2 mmol/L. In the BloomCell framework, this finding is cross-referenced with thyroid markers (Free T3/T4) and blood sugar (HbA1c/Glucose) to identify root cause drivers. Patient education should emphasise that cholesterol elevation is often reversible with anti-inflammatory dietary patterns and targeted supplementation. Narrative language modelled on BloomCell patient communication standards.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell Narrative results.docx - Page 1 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Row 7 | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 7, Columns Maximum/High",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "d5e6f7a8-b9c0-4134-e5fa-6b7c8d9e0f1a",
    "date": "07/10/26 01:55 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Ferritin | Result: 8 ng/mL | Reference: 50–150 ng/mL | Status: CRITICALLY LOW",
    "group": "Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "Ferritin is critically below the BloomCell functional minimum of 50 ng/mL (result: 8 ng/mL), carrying the highest severity and impact score in this panel. This level of iron storage depletion is directly driving the haemoglobin deficiency and must be addressed with urgent supplementation and dietary intervention within the next 30 days.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 22, Columns Minimum/Severity Score/Impact Score",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "e6f7a8b9-c0d1-4245-f6ab-7c8d9e0f1a2b",
    "date": "07/10/26 01:55 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Start iron bisglycinate 25–50 mg daily with vitamin C (500 mg) to enhance absorption — take on an empty stomach or 30 minutes before meals. Increase dietary iron intake: red meat 3–4x/week, lentils, spinach, pumpkin seeds. Avoid tea/coffee within 1 hour of iron supplement. Request a full iron panel (serum iron, TIBC, transferrin saturation) and repeat ferritin in 6 weeks. If ferritin does not respond, consider IV iron infusion referral and coeliac antibody testing (IgA anti-tTG) to rule out malabsorption.",
    "group": "Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "Ferritin at 8 ng/mL is 84% below the BloomCell functional minimum (50 ng/mL), representing the highest-urgency finding in this report. Iron bisglycinate is the preferred form due to lower GI side effects. Vitamin C co-administration is supported by BloomCell Nutrition MVP strategies for iron-deficiency correction. Repeat testing at 6 weeks aligns with BloomCell monitoring protocols. GI and malabsorption workup warranted given severity of depletion without apparent dietary cause documented.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx - Nutrition Strategies sheet - Row 34 | BloomCell Recovery MVP v2.xlsx - Recovery Strategies sheet - Column 15 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Row 22",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "f7a8b9c0-d1e2-4356-a7bc-8d9e0f1a2b3c",
    "date": "07/10/26 01:55 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Vitamin D (25-OH) | Result: 18 nmol/L | Reference: 100–150 nmol/L | Status: SEVERELY DEFICIENT",
    "group": "Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "Vitamin D at 18 nmol/L is severely below the BloomCell functional range of 100–150 nmol/L, representing an 82% deficit. Chronic vitamin D deficiency at this level impairs immune regulation, bone mineralisation, cardiovascular function, and mood — making this a systemic long-term concern requiring sustained repletion and monitoring over 3–6 months.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 15, Columns Minimum/Maximum/Severity Score",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  },
  {
    "id": "a8b9c0d1-e2f3-4467-b8cd-9e0f1a2b3c4d",
    "date": "07/10/26 01:55 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Target Vitamin D (25-OH) >100 nmol/L within 3 months and >125 nmol/L at 6-month review. Begin vitamin D3 5,000–10,000 IU/day with vitamin K2 (MK-7, 100–200 mcg/day) and magnesium glycinate (300–400 mg/day) to support absorption and calcium metabolism. Retest: Vitamin D + PTH at 6–8 weeks; full metabolic panel including calcium and phosphate at 3 months; comprehensive panel at 6 months. If level does not respond to oral supplementation by week 8, assess for fat malabsorption (consider Genova GI-MAP). Long-term: maintain daily sun exposure 20–30 minutes midday (arms and legs) year-round where possible. Annual full panel retest recommended once optimal range achieved.",
    "group": "Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "Vitamin D deficiency of this severity requires aggressive loading protocol before transitioning to maintenance dosing. K2 co-supplementation prevents soft tissue calcification during high-dose D3 repletion. Magnesium is required for vitamin D hydroxylation — deficiency blocks conversion. PTH monitoring ensures calcium homeostasis. GI-MAP is triggered from BloomCell Key Supportive Testing sheet for cases of non-response to oral supplementation, indicating possible malabsorption. Six-month strategy aligns with BloomCell Long-Term Strategy framework for nutrient repletion at severe deficiency levels.",
    "referencesource": "Master Report April 2026 (2).docx - Page 5 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key supportive Testing sheet - Row 11 | BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx - Nutrition Strategies sheet - Row 28 | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 15, Columns Minimum/Maximum",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  }
]
```



Now I'll build the 8 merged records and store each one in Cosmos DB. Proceeding with Step 5 — storing all records one by one.

---

**Storing Record 1 of 8 — Internal Notes (Highlight)**



✅ Record 1 saved. Now storing **Record 2 of 8 — Internal Notes (Note)**✅ Record 2 saved. Now storing **Record 3 of 8 — Understanding Your Results (Highlight)**✅ Record 3 saved. Now storing **Record 4 of 8 — Understanding Your Results (Note)**✅ Record 4 saved. Now storing **Record 5 of 8 — Priority Actions (Highlight)**✅ Record 5 saved. Now storing **Record 6 of 8 — Priority Actions (Note)**✅ Record 6 saved. Now storing **Record 7 of 8 — Long-Term Strategy (Highlight)**✅ Record 7 saved. Now storing **Record 8 of 8 — Long-Term Strategy (Note)**

8 records saved successfully.

---

Now proceeding to **Step 6 — Updating the original PatientDocument** in Cosmos DB.



Document updated successfully.
